name: Cranberry-Orange Bars
recipeInstructions:
substitutions = 2 cups whole fresh or frozen berries instead of 1 cup chopped.
substitutions = 2 Tablespoons fructose instead of brown sugar
1. Combine the cranberries, oranges, raisins, and brown sugar in a mixing bowl. Set aside.
2. Cream the margarine until light and fluffy. Add 1 egg at a time and beat well. Blend in vanilla.
3. Gradually add the flours and baking powder to the creamed mixture. Stir in the cranberry mixture.
4. Pour the batter into a greased 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
5. Bake in a 350°F oven for 30 to 40 minutes, or until browned on top.
6. Cool in the pan on a wire rack.
7. Cut into 24 bars.
From the ADA Holiday cookbook. One serving = 1 bar = 1 fruit exchange = 1/2 fat exchange = 86 calories, 14 carbohydrates, 2 protein, 3 fat, 55 milligrams sodium, 78 potassium, 11 cholesterol.
